svg.text: Where pairwise tracking breaks — triangle-local transport captures structure that pairs miss
Grid: p in {2, 3, 4}, k ≤ 3. 11 local law families.
Status: 2 controls, 5 assignment-only, 0 pair splits, 4 hidden-future winners
3 variables with edges {1-2, 1-3, 2-3} — the simplest structure
Pair-only laws stop at assignment curvature.
Seed quotients: runtime / assignment / pair / simplex on the smallest family showing structure
The pair fiber already agrees — but the future diverges. Only simplex transport sees it.
triangle family at (p=3, k=2) — same witness as the first pair-insufficient split
Why does pair transport miss this?
States u and v agree on every pairwise projection — any pair of variables
that only becomes visible when you compose with w and look at future output.
This is the holonomy: transporting around the triangle reveals hidden state.
pairwise ≠ simplicial. The triangle is the first witness.

g.text: Tie-breaking order: p, k, family_count, pair_alphabet_size, simplex_alphabet_size, reachable_state_count, witness_length, family, semantics.
Reading: broadcast stays flat; pair-only stops at assignment; simplex-local creates the first exact boundary.

svg.text: Figure 4 · Stark: Selective Memory Theory · Pair vs Simplex Holonomy Boundary Search
